description: A six-board premium shortform beat deck: macro hook, sensory lock, reveal, proof, identity reinforcement, then packshot payoff.

storyboard_deck.visual_world: {visual_style}; keep the same premium product world, lighting logic, materials, and set dressing across every board in the sequence
storyboard_deck.layout_system: Designed beat-deck / motionized-carousel layout: short readable copy in the upper safe zone, one hero visual beat per board, clean margins for later motion
storyboard_deck.copy_style: Short sentence-case board copy, one clear beat per board, readable on phone
continuity_locks: Recurring hero subject / product lock across every board: keep the same {product_name} as the hero identity.
Recurring supporting prop / companion cue lock across every board: preserve {supporting_detail} without swapping to a new prop set.
Recurring key object / material detail lock across every board: preserve {primary_sensory_cue} as the same recognizable hero detail.
If a recurring mascot, spokesperson, or character is present in the approved board/reference, keep that same character identity across the full sequence.

shot_templates.intent: Stop-scroll sensory hook that makes the product feel premium.
shot_templates.environment_template: {environment}; controlled tabletop setup with premium material contrast
shot_templates.motion_beat_template: Slow macro drift across the product surface, ending on a tactile detail
shot_templates.camera_language: Extreme close macro, shallow depth of field, crisp specular highlights

reference_requirements.description: Close visual reference for {product_name} material, texture, finish, and premium sensory cue.

notes: Keep the product identity legible even at macro scale.
Avoid extra readable text outside the approved board copy, plus stray labels or UI overlays.

storyboard_board.title: Hook
storyboard_board.layout_notes: Lead with the hook copy high in frame, let the macro texture dominate the board, and keep one tactile focal point with premium negative space.

variation_hints: Try one take with a left-to-right drift.
Try one take with a slow push toward the hero texture.
Try one take with the light catching the product edge.

shot_templates.intent: Lock the recognizable hero material/object detail before the wider reveal.
shot_templates.subject_template: Faithful sensory lock detail of {product_name}, clearly preserving {primary_sensory_cue}
shot_templates.motion_beat_template: Short glide across the locked hero detail while the product identity stays intact
shot_templates.camera_language: Tight detail insert, faithful product geometry, tactile texture clarity

reference_requirements.description: Reference showing the hero material/object detail that must stay consistent across the sequence.

notes: Keep the hero detail faithful to the approved product/object lock.
Do not let the texture/detail crop become abstract or unrecognizable.

storyboard_board.title: Texture
storyboard_board.layout_notes: Keep the copy high and readable, use one locked material/object detail as the hero beat, and leave enough negative space so the board still reads as part of the same designed sequence.

variation_hints: Try one take with the texture catching edge light.
Try one take with a cleaner centered crop.
Try one take with a diagonal macro composition.

shot_templates.intent: Reveal the full hero product while keeping the locked identity intact.
shot_templates.subject_template: Full hero reveal of {product_name}, faithful to the locked product form
shot_templates.motion_beat_template: The full product settles into clear view, then holds for recognition.
shot_templates.camera_language: Medium product reveal, smooth push-in, clean silhouette, clear product read

reference_requirements.description: Reference showing the full product form, silhouette, and locked hero identity that must persist across the sequence.

notes: Keep the product instantly recognizable from the approved board.
Do not drift into a new package, mascot, or object design.

storyboard_board.title: Reveal
storyboard_board.layout_notes: Show the full hero product clearly, keep the copy short at the top, and use the board to reset orientation before the proof/demo beat.

variation_hints: Try one take with a straight-on hero reveal.
Try one take with a slight three-quarter angle.
Try one take with a gentler product settle into frame.

shot_templates.intent: Demonstrate the core benefit in one simple readable beat.
shot_templates.motion_beat_template: Product enters or rotates into view, then performs the simple demo beat
shot_templates.camera_language: Medium close product demo, smooth push-in, clean parallax, readable action

reference_requirements.description: Reference showing the product form factor, demo action, and how {key_benefit} should read visually.

notes: The demo should be visually understandable without captions.
Keep motion simple enough for review and winner selection.

storyboard_board.title: Proof
storyboard_board.layout_notes: Anchor the short proof copy near the top, show the product action clearly in one board, and keep spacing clean so the proof reads fast.

variation_hints: Try one take with a clean hand interaction.
Try one take with the product rotating into the benefit moment.
Try one take with the benefit revealed by lighting change.

shot_templates.intent: Reinforce the signature brand/object cue that should persist across every board.
shot_templates.subject_template: {product_name} anchored by {supporting_detail}, preserving the same hero subject/object set
shot_templates.environment_template: {environment}; clean identity board with room for the supporting hero cue
shot_templates.motion_beat_template: Hold on the signature brand/object cue, then give it a small premium accent move.
shot_templates.camera_language: Controlled medium close framing, strong subject separation, brand-safe clarity

reference_requirements.description: Reference showing the signature mascot, product companion object, or brand cue that must persist through the sequence.

notes: Keep the same brand/object identity from the approved storyboard board.
Do not introduce new supporting props that replace the locked cue.

storyboard_board.title: Identity
storyboard_board.layout_notes: Use one signature supporting cue beside the hero product, keep the board clean and legible, and reinforce the same subject/object set before the final payoff.

variation_hints: Try one take with the supporting cue slightly behind the product.
Try one take with the cue tighter to the hero subject.
Try one take with a cleaner negative-space composition.

shot_templates.intent: Land the product memory with a final hero packshot payoff.
shot_templates.environment_template: {environment}; clean premium end-frame with negative space for review notes
shot_templates.motion_beat_template: Subtle hero push-in, then hold steady for the payoff.
shot_templates.camera_language: Locked hero product angle, polished commercial lighting, stable final frame

reference_requirements.description: Approved hero product reference for final framing, silhouette, color, and brand-safe finish.

notes: Hold the final product identity cleanly for review.
Leave the shot uncluttered for downstream edit decisions.

storyboard_board.title: Payoff
storyboard_board.layout_notes: Use minimal payoff copy near the top or corner, center the hero packshot, and leave clean negative space so the board feels like a premium designed end card.

variation_hints: Try one take with a straight-on hero angle.
Try one take with a three-quarter premium product angle.
Try one take with a slower final push and longer hold.
